ref: to rule day and night, and to separate light from darkness. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: ŋubɔ ń nīn bí ki ŋmíntí ŋwiimpu, ŋun kíǹee ń nīn bí ki ŋmíntí kunyeeu. Unimbɔti ká yii míǹ ŋān̄.
translated: If you are going to the market, bring me some salt. If you are going to the market, bring me some pepper. I will give you money.

ref: Lotan's sons were Hori and Heman; his sister was Timna.
text: Lootan jipɔmbi di sá Oori nì Eemam ní u-niisal sá Tiimina.
translated: Lootan jipɔmbi di sá Oori nì Eemam ní u-niisal sá Tiimina.

ref: Abraham's servant ran to her and said, “Please let me have a drink of water.”
text: Ní utɔntɔnlee sáǹ ki kíntì-u ní ki bí-u yii: «M gbāam̀-si, pɔ̄-mi a-nyim fiii ḿ nyɔ̀.»
translated: He said to them, "I am going to sleep, wake me when it is morning."

text: Ní Biila cáá dipool ní ki màl̄ Jaakɔb kibininjabiki.
translated: Jacob did not give birth to a son.

ref: She was having a rough time, but the woman who was helping her said, “Don't worry! It's a boy.”
text: Ŋyunti ŋun u bíi yáliŋkí imatiŋween ki làá màal̀ee ní umatimmaanli bí-u yii: «Pɔ́ɔǹ a-ba, kibininjabiki ní a làá gíti màal̀.»
translated: “My child, I will tell you something: If you want to be happy, you must be kind.”

ref: to rule day and night, and to separate light from darkness. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
